basic_info.name: Riyue Bay (Sun and Moon Bay) / 萬寧日月灣
basic_info.location: Riyue Bay, Hexin Village (Tianxin Cun area), Wanning City, Hainan Province, China; ~87 km north of Sanya, ~168 km south of Haikou
basic_info.operator: China National Surfing Training Base; surf cluster around Tianxin Village (Hexin) operated by 50+ independent surf clubs and guesthouses; new adjacent PerfectSwell Surf Resort operated by China Tourism Group (CTG) / AWM venue partnership opened Nov 11, 2025
basic_info.opening_date: N/A (natural surf spot; recognized as national surf training base since 2010s)

technical_specs.pool_size: Bay extends ~5-7 km of coastline with multiple breaks (Beachie, Main Point, Ghost Hotel) and roughly 15 left point setups along the NE-E facing shoreline [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_height: Seasonal average 0.6-2.0 m; winter peaks (Oct-Mar) regularly produce 2 m sets, summer/monsoon waves typically 0.5-1.2 m
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Not adjustable (natural ocean swell driven by NE monsoon and typhoon groundswell)
technical_specs.wave_types: Mix of point break (predominantly left-handers at Main Point and Ghost Hotel) and beach break (Beachie zone for beginners); long peeling lefts suit longboard and shortboard intermediate-to-advanced surfers
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: N/A (natural ocean sets, typically 3-6 waves per set with 8-15 second intervals depending on swell period) [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: N/A (open ocean access; surf school lessons typically 90-120 minutes)
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A (natural left-hand point breaks favor goofy-foot frontside; regulars surf backside)
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Predominantly point break (left-handers) with adjacent beach break sections; suits longboards in summer and shortboards/funboards in winter

pricing_sessions.price_range: Free open-ocean access; surf school lessons typically 300-500 RMB for 2-hour group session including board and rashguard; private lessons 500-800 RMB
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: Tiered by surf school: Beginner group lesson ~300 RMB; Intermediate coaching ~500 RMB; Advanced/private 600-800 RMB; competition-level coaching by appointment [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Soft-top board ~80-100 RMB/session (with ~500 RMB deposit) or 150-300 RMB/day for performance shortboards; rashguard ~40 RMB; leash typically included
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: Group lesson 200-400 RMB per person; private 1-on-1 lesson 500-800 RMB for ~2 hours; multi-day camps available at GROM Surf Camp and Shaka Surf Club
pricing_sessions.coaching_packages: Multi-day surf camps (3-5 days) ~1,500-3,500 RMB at major schools (e.g., GROM Surf Camp, Shaka); video analysis and photo packages 200-500 RMB add-on [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Most schools sell punch cards (10-session) at ~2,000-3,500 RMB; no formal bay-wide membership [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Several surf clubs (Shaka, GROM) accept team-building bookings; minimum group sizes typically 10-20 people; pricing on request [uncertain]

experience_guide.best_season: October to March (NE monsoon swell season); peak November-February with ~80% surfable days
experience_guide.best_swell_window: October-March: NE swells 0.8-2.5 m, period 7-12 s, offshore NW winds; secondary typhoon swells possible August-September
experience_guide.crowd_level: Moderate to crowded in peak winter season (Nov-Feb), especially at Main Point during competitions; uncrowded in shoulder months and at Beachie; weekday mornings quietest
experience_guide.booking_method: Walk-in at any beachside surf club; online booking via Dianping (大眾點評), WeChat mini-programs of individual clubs (Shaka, GROM, Hi Surf, etc.), or Trip.com/Ctrip for surf camps
experience_guide.booking_platform: Dianping (大眾點評), Meituan (美團), Xiaohongshu (小紅書) for shop discovery, WeChat mini-programs, Trip.com / Ctrip (攜程)
experience_guide.payment_options: WeChat Pay and Alipay universally accepted; cash accepted at most shops; international credit cards rarely accepted at small clubs (only at PerfectSwell resort and large hotels)
experience_guide.skill_required: All levels welcome; Beachie suits zero-base beginners; Main Point requires intermediate paddling and pop-up; Ghost Hotel for advanced only
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Most surf schools accept ages 6+ for kids' lessons; no formal upper age limit; participants must be able to swim
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 3/5 - several schools offer kids programs and soft-top boards; Beachie sandbar is shallow and beginner-friendly, but no enclosed kids' pool
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Yes - SUP rentals and lessons available at multiple Hexin/Tianxin village clubs
experience_guide.photography_video_service: Photo/video service offered by most clubs; typical pricing 100-300 RMB for session photos, 300-580 RMB for edited video [uncertain]
experience_guide.language_support: Limited English at most local clubs; Shaka Surf Club, GROM Surf Camp, and a few foreign-owned shops have English-speaking instructors; Mandarin dominant
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: No formal adaptive surfing program; beach access is sandy with no boardwalks [uncertain]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: Not required when booking; stance does not affect access (point break is left-hander, surfable by both stances)
experience_guide.safety_notes: Strong rips in winter; lifeguards present at main beach but not all sections; ISA-certified coaches available at top clubs; surf insurance recommended; competition periods may close certain breaks
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Annual host of National Surfing Championships (中國全國衝浪錦標賽); WSL Wanning Corona Cero Pro QS 4000 (Dec 11-17, 2025 - first WSL return since 2020); historical host of WSL Longboard World Championship 2011-2020, Swatch Girls Pro, ISA China Cup, Hainan Open

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Tianxin Village (田新村) and Hexin Village offer 50+ surf hostels and guesthouses (民宿) ranging from 150-500 RMB/night; mid-range resorts on the bay 600-1,500 RMB; Le Meridien Shimei Bay and other 5-star resorts 1,000-3,000 RMB nearby
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: PerfectSwell Riyue Bay Surf Resort (opened Nov 11, 2025) offers themed surf resort rooms; GROM Surf Camp and Shaka Surf Club operate on-site lodges; Forest Inn beachfront accommodation [uncertain pricing]
surroundings.nearby_food: Surf village cafes (GROM Music Bar, Surf House Cafe), Hainanese seafood restaurants, fresh coconut and Wenchang chicken stands, BBQ stalls; per-person spend 50-150 RMB
surroundings.transport: Self-drive via Hainan East Ring Highway (G98); high-speed train to Shenzhou Station (~10 km) or Wanning Station (~30 km) plus taxi/Didi; free local electric shuttle 8:00-18:00 within bay area
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: ~1.5 hours / ~110 km drive from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX) via G98 East Ring Highway; ~3 hours / ~190 km from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); high-speed rail to Shenzhou Station ~15 minutes from bay
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Riyue Bay surf + Shenzhou Peninsula (神州半島) resort + Xinglong Tropical Botanical Garden (興隆熱帶植物園) + Shimei Bay (石梅灣); 3-5 day Wanning surf trip itinerary common

pros: China's premier surf destination with most consistent winter swell (~200+ surfable days/year)
Diverse breaks suit all levels: Beachie for beginners, Main Point for intermediate, Ghost Hotel for advanced
Dense surf cluster of 50+ clubs in Tianxin Village creates affordable, vibrant surf community
National training base credibility; ISA/WSL-sanctioned competitions hosted annually
Warm water year-round (avg 26.5C), no wetsuit needed most of the year
cons: Crowds can be heavy at Main Point in peak winter and during competitions
Strong rips in winter pose risk for inexperienced surfers
Limited English support outside flagship schools
Travel time from Sanya/Haikou (1.5-3 hours) discourages day trips
Summer surf inconsistent except during typhoon swells; some flat days
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Trip.com / Tripadvisor: ~4.0-4.5/5 average; Xiaohongshu (小紅書) heavily features Riyue Bay as top mainland surf spot; Dianping reviews praise wave consistency and surf-village vibe but note crowding and basic guesthouse standards
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Intermediate to advanced surfers seeking genuine ocean waves; surf-trip travelers; competition viewers; longboarders (long lefts); surf-camp groups
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: China National Surfing Team training base (under General Administration of Sport of China); WSL international event partnership (returned 2025); ISA China Cup partner; new PerfectSwell resort developed by China Tourism Group (CTG) using American Wave Machines (AWM) PerfectSwell technology
